Patch Set 2:

(1 comment)

Rather than work around this problem in the test script, it would be better to handle this case directly in "lctl pool_new" so that it doesn't try to add a duplicate pool.  

Then, instead of just fixing the test scripts, you've also avoided problems for the end user as well.

Patch Set 2:

> (1 comment)
 > 
 > Rather than work around this problem in the test script, it would
 > be better to handle this case directly in "lctl pool_new" so that
 > it doesn't try to add a duplicate pool.
 > 
 > Then, instead of just fixing the test scripts, you've also avoided
 > problems for the end user as well.

As per current implementation POOL_NEW adds record into log file, there is no such ldiskfs functions available to check duplicate record. 

It is possible to handle in code 
for that i have to write handler to check all records from lustre-client/lustre=MDT<nnnn> log file to check record with command POOL_NEW for that pool name before writing duplicate POOL_NEW with the help of log marker comment.

but problem is listing pool on MGS, listing pool will be complex through log because again i have to write different handler to parse the logfile with command POOL_NEW, POOL_REMOVE, POOL_DESTROY to check the sequence so its little bit complex.

For now we can go ahead with this patch, I will come with patch which checks duplicate pool exist at MGS side through code and will remove check from create_pool wrapper.

Patch Set 3:

It looks like the two options produce slightly different results - the first one prevents insertion of the duplicate entry into the log, while the second only returns an error during log processing if it hits a duplicate log.  It would be preferable to prevent the duplicate pool to be added to the log, rather than handling it after the fact.  That said, if there are sites that already have duplicate pool names in the logs, then this needs to be handled gracefully (i.e. not abort config log processing).

My original through was to have "lctl pool_new <arg>" do an internal call to "llapi_poollist(argv[1])" to see if the pool already exists, instead of complicating the kernel code more, but this won't work if the MGS is on a separate node.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

It appears that this patch is also introducing some test regression, as it has failed every test session with one or more failures, and all of the failures are related to pools.

Patch Set 3:

patch http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/23184/ created to prevent to write duplicate pool log record to log file.

Abandoned

As per comment this need to be handle in code path

parents.subject: LU-8668 tests: print more information about dd failure

commit.subject: LU-8684 tests: modify create_pool to use as wrapper
commit.message:
LU-8684 tests: modify create_pool to use as wrapper

modification to create_pool() which will check pool_list from MDS
before creating pool_new on MGS to avoid attempt to create existing
pool when MGS are separate. Changed the required places which directly
call lctl pool_new to use create_pool() wrapper.

Seagate-bug-id: MRP-2683
Signed-off-by: Jadhav Vikram <jadhav.vikram@seagate.com>
Change-Id: I6e5f6bce7c8ec12109b6efaed337d9e20281f5a5
Reviewed-on: http://es-gerrit.xyus.xyratex.com:8080/11162
Reviewed-by: Vladimir Saveliev <vladimir.saveliev@seagate.com>
Reviewed-by: Rahul Deshmukh <rahul.deshmukh@seagate.com>
Tested-by: Elena V. Gryaznova <elena.gryaznova@seagate.com>
